mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence increases.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive over a 3-month period, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a critical threshold, it signals significant economic distress, leading to reduced consumer demand and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 6.0% for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: An environment of rising inflation coupled with an aggressive federal funds rate hike cycle indicates monetary tightening aimed at curbing price pressures, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.5% over 3 months AND CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3%
failure_condition: If the specified FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery, an acceleration in retail sales indicates a rebound in consumer confidence and spending, which directly translates into stronger corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economy normalizes.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows sustained growth during a recovery regime, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production signals a broad-based contraction in manufacturing and mining output, indicating weakening economic fundamentals that negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines as specified, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent strength in housing starts reflects healthy construction activity and broader economic confidence, acting as a leading indicator for future economic growth and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a positive trend over a 6-month period, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is decelerating while the federal funds rate remains elevated, it indicates a challenging economic environment where monetary policy is restrictive despite slowing demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth rate declines for 2 consecutive quarters AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth slows while FEDFUNDS is high, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of low inflation and accommodative monetary policy, low interest rates reduce the cost of capital and boost corporate valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors seek higher yields in equities.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met in an accommodative regime, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.
